title: Undercut-overcut and traffic-window features from race-relative ordering
summary: Public F1 strategy explanations repeatedly center pit timing on gap evolution, clean air, and whether a rival can undercut or overcut.
rationale: Public F1 strategy explanations repeatedly center pit timing on gap evolution, clean air, and whether a rival can undercut or overcut. Your current features barely model local traffic geometry, yet available columns (`Position`, `LapTime_Delta`, `Position_Change`, `LapNumber`) can approximate who is boxed into traffic or has a clean-air incentive to stop now.
implementation_hint: Within each `Race x Year x LapNumber`, compute leakage-safe relative features: percentile/rank of lap-time delta, tyre life, and degradation; gaps to nearby positions using same-lap lap-time surrogates; indicators for being stuck behind slower traffic; and regime flags for plausible undercut vs overcut opportunities by comparing a driver’s current pace deterioration to the race-lap median and nearby-position cohort.
expected_effect: Adds tactical signal beyond raw tyre wear, especially for cases where pitting is triggered by rival pressure or traffic release rather than absolute degradation.
risk: Without true time gaps, proxy features can be noisy; race-lap aggregates must use only same-row contemporaneous information, not future laps.
